title: dalbergia greveana
intro: Dalbergia greveana, also known as Madagascar ebony or rosewood, is a species of tree in the legume family Fabaceae. It is native to Madagascar and is highly valued for its timber and medicinal properties. The extracts of Dalbergia greveana have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tree's wood and leaves are used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections.
